footer.description: An independent non-profit organization dedicated to the affairs of Syrians residing in the Netherlands

about.title: About Us
about.subtitle: Syrian Community in the Netherlands
about.description: The Syrian Community in the Netherlands is an independent non-profit organization dedicated to the affairs of Syrians residing in the Netherlands, aiming to unite their voices, support them, and strengthen their sense of belonging.
about.visionDesc: We aspire to build a cohesive, active, and integrated Syrian community that serves as a positive model of integration without assimilation, preserving its cultural and national identity while being open to Dutch society. Our vision is a community that inspires, participates, and contributes to building a better future for its members.
about.missionDesc: Our mission is to be a unifying voice for the Syrian community in the Netherlands, a gathering space that expresses their aspirations and strengthens their sense of belonging. We work to support community members in various aspects of their lives and facilitate their integration into Dutch society while preserving their cultural identity.
about.valueBelongingDesc: We believe diversity is strength, and belonging does not negate openness
about.valueTeamworkDesc: We work with a team spirit and treat everyone with respect, equality, and transparency
about.valueNeutralityDesc: We are committed to neutrality, oppose all forms of discrimination, and support freedom of opinion and human dignity
about.valueTransparencyDesc: We ensure absolute transparency in all activities, free from political or personal agendas
about.goal1: Legal representation and advocacy for community members
about.goal2: Promoting cultural identity and the Arabic language
about.goal3: Academic and professional empowerment of students and immigrants
about.goal4: Social and humanitarian solidarity for the most needy groups

regulations.title: Internal Regulations
regulations.description: The internal regulations document defines the identity, objectives, working mechanisms, and administrative structure of the Syrian Community in the Netherlands.
regulations.body: The internal regulations document defines the identity, objectives, working mechanisms, and administrative structure of the Syrian Community in the Netherlands. It reflects our commitment to transparency, neutrality, and organized collective work, and serves as a fundamental reference for all members and affiliates in understanding their rights and duties.
regulations.identityDesc: Defines the vision, mission, and values of the Syrian Community in the Netherlands.
regulations.governanceDesc: Mechanisms for forming the board of directors, working committees, and distribution of administrative responsibilities.
regulations.rightsDesc: Clarifies the rights and duties of affiliated members and officials in the community.
regulations.membershipDesc: Terms and procedures for registration, withdrawal, and voting in the general assembly.
regulations.downloadDesc: To promote transparency and provide an opportunity for all members, candidates, and those wishing to join, you can view and download the full internal regulations of the Syrian Community in the Netherlands in PDF format.

whatsappMessages.accepted:
Hello {name} 🎉

We are pleased to inform you that your membership application to the Syrian Community in the Netherlands has been approved. You are now an official member of the community!

Thank you for joining us 💚
whatsappMessages.rejected:
Sorry {name}

Thank you for your interest in joining the Syrian Community in the Netherlands. Unfortunately, your membership application could not be approved at this time.
whatsappMessages.contactPrompt: You can contact us for more information.
whatsappMessages.confirmation:
Hello {name} 🙏

Thank you for submitting your membership application to the Syrian Community in the Netherlands. Your application is being reviewed, and we will notify you once a decision is made.

Best regards, Community Team 💚
whatsappMessages.pendingReminder:
Hi {name} 👋

Just a reminder that your membership application for the Syrian Community in the Netherlands is still under review. If you have any questions, feel free to contact us.
